meta.methodology: Every dupe is checked against real reviews from multiple independent sources. A dupe is only shown if it has consistent positive reviews (composite ≈ 4/5 or better, multiple sources, no major durability or safety red flags). Fake-branded / counterfeit items are never listed — only legitimate affordable alternatives. Dupes that fail the check are listed in 'Didn't make the cut' with the reason.
meta.disclaimer: Prices are approximate and change often — verify before buying. Not affiliated with any brand or retailer; no affiliate links. 'Dupe' here means a legitimate lower-cost alternative with a similar look/effect, never a counterfeit.

dupes.summary: A genuinely close lit-from-within glow for under a third of the price — even a touch more hydrating thanks to hyaluronic acid.
dupes.caveat: Oily skin: set with powder or you may go shiny by afternoon. Slightly thicker, needs a little more blending.

reviews.takeaway: "A pretty good dupe — not 100% identical, but Charlotte Tilbury isn't twice as good, and this is less than half the price."
Recommends e.l.f. for value + a dewier, more hydrated finish; CT for a more refined glow.

dupes.summary: Reviewers find staying power essentially equal — the e.l.f. is actually stickier and grips makeup all day for a third of the cost.
dupes.caveat: Very tacky on application (it does dry down). Alcohol-based vs Milk's more hydrating formula.

reviews.takeaway: "The two primers compare equally in staying power." $11 vs $38.
e.l.f. layers best with skincare + makeup and is named the winner on value.

dupes.summary: Near-identical full-coverage, matte-dry-down concealer for ~$6. Many reviewers find it blends with less creasing than Shape Tape.
dupes.caveat: Oxidizes slightly darker than the cap shade; 18 shades vs Tarte's 30. Pick a touch lighter.

reviews.takeaway: "Performance and coverage are almost identical" — both full-coverage, creamy, matte.
Builds beautifully and blends just as easily, if not more so, than Shape Tape.

dupes.summary: Cork-footbed sandal with real arch support and — unlike Birkenstocks — basically no break-in period. Comfortable out of the box.
dupes.caveat: Runs small (size up a half). Less durable long-term than a Birkenstock.

reviews.takeaway: Immediate comfort + arch support, no break-in — a "fantastic, affordable alternative."
Highly recommended for those new to structured footbeds wanting arch support.

dupes.summary: A cushiony, plush everyday trainer with a cult following — a comfy, sleeker stand-in for Hoka's max-cushion ride at a fraction of the price.
dupes.caveat: More of a lifestyle/walking shoe; serious runners may still prefer Hoka, Brooks Glycerin or ASICS Nimbus for high mileage.

reviews.takeaway: Listed among Hoka alternatives that "feel just as good," from $48.
Cushioned, soft step with a heavy dose of comfort and a loyal following.

dupes.summary: Soft, well-constructed grade-A cashmere for ~$50 — widely praised as luxury-level quality at a fraction of designer prices.
dupes.caveat: Some pilling and occasional fit inconsistency reported — generally seen as acceptable for the price.

reviews.takeaway: Broadly positive: soft, holds up after washes, excellent quality-to-price ratio.

dupes.summary: Strikingly similar: same size, water-resistant fabric, adjustable strap and pockets — with 10,000+ 5-star reviews and 30+ colors.
dupes.caveat: The Lululemon will outlast it long-term; zipper hardware differs (metal vs plastic).

reviews.takeaway: "You can't tell me this doesn't look the exact same" — ~$14 vs $38.
Same size + water-resistant fabric; main diff is metal vs plastic zipper.

dupes.summary: A conditional pass: 20k+ ratings and ~82% at 4 stars or higher. Gets you ~70% of the way for ~30% of the price.
dupes.caveat: Polyester (vs SKIMS' softer polyamide). Some find it compresses hard, runs short in the torso, and may shrink. Buy true to size, not for daily heavy wear.

reviews.takeaway: Worthy budget alternative, but not identical — SKIMS hand-feel and durability are real.
A solid ~$29 alternative when SKIMS is sold out.

dupes.summary: Mimics Dyson's most-loved features — strong suction, ~40 min runtime, converts to a handheld — for a fraction of the cost.
dupes.caveat: Runtime/peak suction below the top Dyson; owners report 3–5 yr lifespans with care.

reviews.takeaway: "Best alternative to Dyson stick vacuums" — mimics coveted features for a fraction of the cost.
Listed among the best Dyson alternatives that cost less without sacrificing core performance.

dupes.summary: A 4.3 lb cordless that converts to handheld and earned a hands-on 'Dyson dupe to contend with' verdict — around $149–179.
dupes.caveat: Smaller brand; check warranty terms. Not as refined as Dyson's flagship.

reviews.takeaway: "My house is spotless thanks to this $149 Dyson vacuum clone."
Among the best Dyson cordless alternatives according to owners.

dupes.summary: Rejected. A bag that copies a brand's logo/monogram is a counterfeit — a legal gray area, and reviewers consistently report poor materials and hardware.
dupes.caveat: We only list legitimate look-alikes, never fakes that carry someone else's trademark.

reviews.takeaway: Designer logo 'dupes' carry real quality and authenticity concerns.

dupes.summary: Rejected. The sub-$70 no-name clones repeatedly draw complaints of weak suction and short lifespans — the reputable budget picks (Tineco, Ultenic, Shark) are only a little more.
dupes.caveat: Spend the extra ~$90 on a reviewed brand; the rock-bottom clones are a false economy.

reviews.takeaway: Affordable alternatives can be great — but the cheapest no-name clones are where quality falls apart.

dupes.summary: Rejected for running. Sub-$25 no-name 'Hoka-style' shoes look the part but reviewers report the foam packs out fast and offers little real support — fine for casual wear, not for mileage.
dupes.caveat: If you actually run, step up to Adidas Cloudfoam, Brooks or ASICS; the $20 ones aren't worth the injury risk.

reviews.takeaway: Plenty of good Hoka alternatives exist — but the rock-bottom no-names aren't among the ones that 'feel just as good.'
